content.text:
Implemented the ARM relocation fix.

What changed:
- In `librz/bin/format/elf/elf_relocs_conversion.c`, `R_ARM_GLOB_DAT` and `R_ARM_JUMP_SLOT` now use plain `ADD(...)` instead of `ADD_T(...)`.
- This prevents ARM Thumb LSB tagging from being applied to dynamic data relocations such as `__stack_chk_guard`, which caused names/targets to resolve incorrectly (e.g. the `+ 1` target issue that could let `aav` flags win over the proper relocation symbol).

Validation performed:
- Rebuilt successfully with:
  - `meson compile -C /testbed/build`
- Verified `__stack_chk_guard` relocation display on an existing ARM sample:
  - `R_ARM_GLOB_DAT __stack_chk_guard` now has no `+ 0x00000001`
  - disassembly at the relocation still shows `reloc.target.__stack_chk_guard` / `__stack_chk_guard`
- Ran ARM ELF relocation regression tests:
  - `"/testbed/build/binrz/rz-test/rz-test" -q "/testbed/test/db/formats/elf/elf-relarm"`
  - Result: `7 OK`, `5 BR`, `0 XX`, `0 FX` (existing broken tests remain broken; no new failures)

Cleanup:
- Did not leave added test files.
- Restored unrelated modified regression-test file (`test/integration/meson.build`).
- Final working tree change is only:
  - `librz/bin/format/elf/elf_relocs_conversion.c`
